justification: All standard ADR sections present (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered). Crash-recovery lock behavior explicitly described in both Rationale and Trade-offs sections. All three alternatives listed: Redis SETNX, ZooKeeper, and custom heartbeat table. Context clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem. No invented information. ADR is readable as a standalone document.

observations.text: Excellent ADR structure with clear separation of Rationale, Alternatives, Trade-offs, and Consequences
Crash-recovery semantics thoroughly covered: session-level lock release on connection drop, plus the hung-but-alive edge case
Alternatives table includes rejection rationale for each option, not just a list
Trade-offs section adds operational nuance (no external visibility, no TTL, single-DB assumption) beyond what was strictly required
Actionable forward-looking guidance (alerting for missed runs, revisit if Redis adopted) without inventing context
The handoff_brief format preceding the ADR is not part of the ADR itself — adds noise, though the ADR portion is self-contained
